Table and Field Management on PostgreSQL

Table Creation

Every new database contains a public schema.

Tables are created in the schema specified by the assignment file, either on the fly or by scripts generated by the Create Table facility. It is recommended that you use the Create Table facility before going to a production environment with your application, because database triggers are not created for tables that are created on the fly.

When you first attempt to access a table, Uniface needs to know if it exists. If the table does not exist, Uniface creates it and its indexes, using the information from the application model.

Tables and procedures are created with the following permissions:

  • grant delete, insert, references, select, update on table to public

  • grant execute is issued on the stored procedures

Table and Field Names

Observer the following general rules for table and field names:

  • If possible, follow Uniface naming rules for entity names:
    • Letters (A-Z), numbers (0-9), and underscores (_) are allowed.
    • The first character must be a letter.
    • Uniface accepts only uppercase names in the application model.
  • Avoid using DBMS reserved words for the names of entities or fields.
  • Table names must be unique in the first 21 characters.

Uniface table and field names are case-insensitive, so Uniface treats ABCD, abcd, and abCD all as the same table name—as ABCD.

The PGS connector creates all table and field names in lowercase or leaves them unconverted.

The names of stored procedures and triggers are created using the table name as a base. If the table name is longer than 21 characters, the trigger or procedure name is created using only the first 21 characters of the table name.

Segmented Fields

Segmented fields are supported. For more information, see Segmented Fields: String Packing Codes (SC and SU).

Overflow Tables

Overflow tables are created on the fly when needed. An overflow table contains the primary key from the base table, and the overflow information from the field which causes the overflow table. The name for an overflow table consists of the same name as the lowercase entity name with the prefix o.

For entities with only fixed length fields the maximum record size is 2GB-1. The maximum record size for entities that have variable-length fields is 8KB, the rest goes into the overflow table. For example, when a C* field is encountered, a standard table with a length of 7989 characters is created; an overflow table is created only when this number of characters is exceeded.

Note: The overflow mechanism is a technical encoding for larger amounts of data, and is not transparent to other applications. For more information, see Overflow Tables.

Entities that contain one of the following Uniface packing codes require both a base table and an overflow table:

  • C*
  • R*
  • U*
  • W*
  • VC*
  • VR*
  • VU*
  • VW*
Related Links
  • Overflow Tables

    DBMSs may impose upper limits to the number of fields in a table, the length of a field, and the length of a record. Some DBMSs do not support variable-length fields with a high maximum length. To overcome these limitations, Uniface provides an overflow mechanism, in which data that is represented as a single modeled entity is automatically split into two tables—the base table and an overflow table.
